Module com.example.miniproyectosudoku6x6
Package com.example.miniproyectosudoku6x6.model

Class TableroSudoku

java.lang.Object
com.example.miniproyectosudoku6x6.model.TableroSudoku
public class TableroSudoku extends Object
Represents the complete 6x6 Sudoku board.

The board is internally organized as a 6x6 matrix of Celda objects, and also exposes logical groupings of those cells as rows, columns and 2x3 blocks. These groupings make rule validation and helper operations easier to express in later phases.

The 6x6 grid is divided into 6 blocks of 2 rows by 3 columns:

  +---------+---------+
  | Block 0 | Block 1 |   (rows 0-1)
  +---------+---------+
  | Block 2 | Block 3 |   (rows 2-3)
  +---------+---------+
  | Block 4 | Block 5 |   (rows 4-5)
  +---------+---------+

This class belongs to the model layer and is completely independent of JavaFX or any UI framework.

    Description
    private final List<List<Celda>>
    bloques
    Logical grouping of cells by 2x3 block.

    • matrizCeldas

      private final Celda[][] matrizCeldas
      Two-dimensional matrix holding all cells of the board.

    • filas

      private final List<List<Celda>> filas
      Logical grouping of cells by row. Each inner list contains 6 cells.

    • columnas

      private final List<List<Celda>> columnas
      Logical grouping of cells by column. Each inner list contains 6 cells.

    • bloques

      private final List<List<Celda>> bloques
      Logical grouping of cells by 2x3 block. Each inner list contains 6 cells.

  • Constructor Details

    • TableroSudoku

      public TableroSudoku()
      Constructs a new empty Sudoku board. All 36 cells are created as empty and the row, column and block groupings are populated for fast access.

  • Method Details

    • inicializarCeldas

      private void inicializarCeldas()
      Creates every cell of the matrix as an empty cell.

    • inicializarAgrupaciones

      private void inicializarAgrupaciones()
      Builds the logical lists that group cells by row, column and block. After this method runs, every cell of the board is referenced from exactly one row list, one column list and one block list.

    • calcularIndiceBloque

      public static int calcularIndiceBloque(int fila, int columna)
      Computes the block index for the cell located at the given coordinates.

      The 6x6 board has 6 blocks arranged in a 3x2 layout (3 block-rows by 2 block-columns), where each block is 2 rows by 3 columns.

      Parameters:
      fila - the row index (0-5)
      columna - the column index (0-5)
      Returns:
      the block index (0-5)

    • obtenerCelda

      public Celda obtenerCelda(int fila, int columna)
      Returns the cell located at the given coordinates.
      Parameters:
      fila - the row index (0-5)
      columna - the column index (0-5)
      Returns:
      the cell at that position
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the coordinates are out of range

    • obtenerFila

      public List<Celda> obtenerFila(int fila)
      Returns the list of cells that belong to the given row.
      Parameters:
      fila - the row index (0-5)
      Returns:
      a list containing the 6 cells of that row

    • obtenerColumna

      public List<Celda> obtenerColumna(int columna)
      Returns the list of cells that belong to the given column.
      Parameters:
      columna - the column index (0-5)
      Returns:
      a list containing the 6 cells of that column

    • obtenerBloque

      public List<Celda> obtenerBloque(int indiceBloque)
      Returns the list of cells that belong to the given 2x3 block.
      Parameters:
      indiceBloque - the block index (0-5)
      Returns:
      a list containing the 6 cells of that block

    • estaCompleto

      public boolean estaCompleto()
      Checks whether every cell in the board has a value assigned.
      Returns:
      true if no cell is empty, false otherwise

    • limpiarTablero

      public void limpiarTablero()
      Clears the value of every non-fixed cell in the board.

    • validarCoordenadas

      private void validarCoordenadas(int fila, int columna)
      Validates that the given coordinates are within the board boundaries.
      Parameters:
      fila - the row index to validate
      columna - the column index to validate
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if any coordinate is out of range

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a human-readable representation of the board, useful for debugging and console testing. Empty cells are shown as ".".
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      a multi-line string with the current state of the board
